fields.description:
# op-cert: docs + ADR (env-attribution, postures, integrity, producer signing, HMAC removal)

## Context
Document the operation-certificate model and its security contract as a named ADR artifact
`docs/adr/NNNN-opcert-asymmetric.md` (pick the next free ADR number), cross-referencing ADR 0046
(security posture; L2 no-transparency-log and L4 single-environment/no-threshold are
op-cert-scoped). The ADR documents the **settled** model from the epic's two design-resolution
sections, including the round-6 producer-signing resolution: uniform producer signing (every
environment signs with its own auto-generated Ed25519 key at `<tracker>/.opcert-key`; the trusted
server is an ordinary environment whose key is pinned out-of-band and which fetches authoritative
state), per-kind native verdict vocabularies with the transport-status/verdict split in the remote
job API, signer identity as deployment config (no repo-config `env_id` key; consumer policy =
`verify.require_environment` + `verify.opcert_enforce_since`; client routing =
`verify.opcert_remote_url`), the Fargate + API Gateway SigV4 + async-job runtime choice (Lambda
and the shared review-bot VM rejected, with reasons), the stateless store-read-only server
(client persists the self-authenticating envelope), and HMAC removal via expand-contract
(`docs/migrations/hmac-opcert-removal.md`).

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `docs/adr/NNNN-opcert-asymmetric.md` exists and is non-empty, covering: the
      environment-attribution model, low/high-security postures, the authoritative-state-fetch
      integrity requirement, the out-of-band trust root (`.rebar/trusted_environments.yaml`),
      merge-gate enforcement, the authoritative era anchor (storage-position key validity; rotation-with-pin vs kill-switch revocation), uniform producer signing + local key provenance, the trusted-server
      runtime + async job API + stateless/read-only posture, and HMAC removal + migration.
- [ ] The ADR cross-references ADR 0046 and contains the strings `rebar.opcert.v1`,
      `trusted_environments.yaml`, and `ADR 0046`; a test asserts the file exists, is non-empty,
      and contains those strings.
